---
title: "Populism and Stereotypes of Civil Servants — Wave 2 Report"
subtitle: "Spontaneous stereotypes, scale stereotypes, and politicization perceptions, linked to wave-1 populism measures"
author: "Saar Alon-Barkat"
date: "February 2025"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
---

# About this report

Wave 2 fielded the stereotype battery and a politicization battery to participants who returned from wave 1, with random assignment to one of three labels for the target civil-servant group (`employee`, `bureaucrat`, `clerk`). This report links those wave-2 outcomes to the wave-1 populism scales, partisanship, ideology, demographics, and trust in the civil service.

# Setup

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(gtsummary)
library(sjPlot)
library(haven)
library(labelled)
library(codebook)
library(janitor)
library(lavaan)
library(psych)
```

Import data.

```{r}
load(".RData")
```


# Sample

## Build labelled demographic variables

```{r}
# Build readable string labels for demographic variables on the combined-waves data.
# NOTE: religiosity == 2 and religiosity == 3 are both mapped to "4.Religious".
# This collapses two response categories into one — verify this is intentional.
waves_combined_labs <- waves_combined %>% remove_labels() %>%
  mutate(
    gender_label = case_when(
      gender == 1 ~ "Male",
      gender == 2 ~ "Female",
      gender == 3 ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    age_group_label = case_when(
      age_group == 1 ~ "18-24",
      age_group == 2 ~ "25-34",
      age_group == 3 ~ "35-44",
      age_group == 4 ~ "45-54",
      age_group == 5 ~ "55-64",
      age_group == 6 ~ "65+",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    education_label = case_when(
      education == 1 ~ "1.Elementary or less",
      education == 2 ~ "2.No high school diploma",
      education == 3 ~ "3.High school diploma",
      education == 4 ~ "4.Post high school",
      education == 5 ~ "5.College education (no degree)",
      education == 6 ~ "6.Bachelor's degree",
      education == 7 ~ "7.Master's degree",
      education == 8 ~ "8.Doctorate degree",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    income_label = case_when(
      income == 1 ~ "1.Much below average",
      income == 2 ~ "2.Below average",
      income == 3 ~ "3.Average",
      income == 4 ~ "4.Above average",
      income == 5 ~ "5.Much above average",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sector_employment_label = case_when(
      sector_employment == 1 ~ "Public sector",
      sector_employment == 2 ~ "Private sector",
      sector_employment == 3 ~ "Third sector",
      sector_employment == 4 ~ "Self-employed",
      sector_employment == 5 ~ "Not employed",
      sector_employment == 6 ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    religiosity_label = case_when(
      religiosity == 1 ~ "5.Ultra Orthodox (Charedi)",
      religiosity == 2 ~ "4.Religious",
      religiosity == 3 ~ "4.Religious",  # FLAG: same label as religiosity == 2 — verify
      religiosity == 4 ~ "3.Traditional religious",
      religiosity == 5 ~ "2.Traditional not religious",
      religiosity == 6 ~ "1.Secular",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
```

## Sample demographics — overall

```{r}
# Overall demographic summary across the combined-waves sample.
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2,
                duration_in_minutes, duration_in_minutes_w2, public_service_work_information) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    label = list(
      gender_label ~ "Gender",
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 1. Combined-waves sample — overall demographics**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Sample demographics — by return-to-wave-2

```{r}
# Compares respondents who returned to wave 2 vs. attrited.
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, duration_in_minutes_w2, public_service_work_information) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = return_wave2,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 2. Demographics by return-to-wave-2 status**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Sample demographics — by completion of stereotype battery

```{r}
# Compares respondents who completed the wave-2 stereotype battery vs. those who did not.
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2, duration_in_minutes_w2) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = completed_stereotypes_wave2,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 3. Demographics by completion of stereotype battery**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

```{r}
# Same comparison restricted/expanded to those who completed the stereotype battery
# (kept here for cross-check; original report ran this twice).
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2, duration_in_minutes_w2) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = completed_stereotypes_wave2,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 3b. Demographics by completion of stereotype battery (cross-check)**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Sample demographics — by completion of wave 2

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2,
                duration_in_minutes, duration_in_minutes_w2, public_service_work_information) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = completed_wave2,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 4. Demographics by completion of wave 2**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Duration of wave 2

```{r}
# Histogram of wave-2 completion time, truncated at 30 minutes.
waves_combined %>%
  mutate(duration_in_minutes_w2 = ifelse(duration_in_minutes_w2 > 30, 30, duration_in_minutes_w2)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = duration_in_minutes_w2)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of wave-2 completion time",
       x = "Duration in minutes (truncated at 30)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
```


# Random assignment to civil-servant labels

Wave 2 randomly assigned each respondent to one of three labels for the target group (e.g., *public service employee*, *public service bureaucrat*, *public service clerk*). The table below verifies that random assignment produced balanced groups across demographics and partisanship.

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2,
                duration_in_minutes, duration_in_minutes_w2,
                civil_servant_label, entity_2, entity_3) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = civil_servant_label,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 5. Balance check — covariates by randomly assigned civil-servant label**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```


# Stereotype scales

Eight bipolar stereotype scales (friendly, sociable, trustworthy, fair, competent, skilled, assertive, goal-oriented), plus a global image rating, plus the new wave-2 elite-perception measure (`civil_servant_elite_wave2`). Theoretically, these load onto two higher-order dimensions: **warmth** (sociability + morality) and **competence** (ability + assertiveness).

## Distributions of stereotype scales by label

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(friendly_scale:general_image_social, civil_servant_elite_wave2, civil_servant_label) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = civil_servant_label,
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 6. Stereotype scales by experimental label**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Correlations among stereotype scales and trust

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(friendly_scale:general_image_social, civil_servant_elite_wave2,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, trust_civil_service) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Correlations: stereotype scales × trust in civil service")
```

```{r}
# Correlations between elite-perception (wave 1 binary, wave 2 continuous) and stereotype scales.
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(civil_servant_elite_wave2, elite_group_civil_servants,
                high_status_scale:traditional) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Correlations: elite-perception × status / traditional stereotype scales")
```

## Confirmatory factor analyses

### 2-factor model — Warmth vs. Competence

```{r}
cfa.model.2factor_ster <- '
  communion =~ friendly_scale + sociable_scale + trustworthy_scale + fair_scale
  agency    =~ competent_scale + skilled_scale + assertive_scale + goal_oriented_scale'

fit.cfa.model.2factor_ster <- cfa(
  cfa.model.2factor_ster,
  data   = wave2,
  se     = "robust",
  std.lv = T
)

summary(fit.cfa.model.2factor_ster, fit.measures = TRUE, standardize = TRUE)
```

### 4-factor model — Sociability, Morality, Ability, Assertiveness

```{r}
cfa.model.4factor_ster <- '
  Sociability   =~ friendly_scale + sociable_scale
  Morality      =~ trustworthy_scale + fair_scale
  Ability       =~ competent_scale + skilled_scale
  Assertiveness =~ assertive_scale + goal_oriented_scale'

fit.cfa.model.4factor_ster <- cfa(
  cfa.model.4factor_ster,
  data   = wave2,
  se     = "robust",
  std.lv = T
)

summary(fit.cfa.model.4factor_ster, fit.measures = TRUE, standardize = TRUE)
```

### Hierarchical: 4 first-order factors → 2 second-order factors

```{r}
cfa.model.4_2factor_ster <- '
  Sociability   =~ friendly_scale + sociable_scale
  Morality      =~ trustworthy_scale + fair_scale
  Ability       =~ competent_scale + skilled_scale
  Assertiveness =~ assertive_scale + goal_oriented_scale

  Warmth     =~ Sociability + Morality
  Competence =~ Ability + Assertiveness'

fit.cfa.model.4_2factor_ster <- cfa(
  cfa.model.4_2factor_ster,
  data   = wave2,
  se     = "robust",
  std.lv = T
)

summary(fit.cfa.model.4_2factor_ster, fit.measures = TRUE, standardize = TRUE)
```


# Politicization items

Six items measuring perceived politicization of civil servants. Items 3–5 form the analytical scale (see item-scale diagnostics below).

## Distribution of politicization items — overall

```{r}
wave2 %>% labelled::to_factor() |>
  dplyr::select(q41_1:q41_6, trust_civil_service_wave2, civil_servant_label) %>%
  tbl_summary(missing_text = "Missing") %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 7. Politicization items (q41_1–q41_6) — overall**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Distribution of politicization items — by coalition camp

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>% labelled::to_factor() |>
  dplyr::select(politicization_1:politicization_6, trust_civil_service_wave2,
                coalition_party_vote_intention) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = "coalition_party_vote_intention",
              missing_text = "Missing") %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 8. Politicization items by coalition camp**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```

## Inter-item correlations

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(politicization_1:politicization_6, trust_civil_service_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Correlations: politicization items × trust in civil service")
```

## Item-scale diagnostics

```{r}
# Full 4-item scale (politicization_3 through politicization_6)
wave2 |> labelled::remove_labels() |>
  dplyr::select(politicization_3:politicization_6) |>
  sjPlot::tab_itemscale()

# Reduced 3-item scale (politicization_3 through politicization_5)
wave2 |> labelled::remove_labels() |>
  dplyr::select(politicization_3:politicization_5) |>
  sjPlot::tab_itemscale()
```


# Direction of spontaneous stereotypes

The core wave-2 dependent variable: respondents wrote open-ended associations about civil servants, which were coded for direction (1 = negative, 2 = neutral, 3 = positive) and content (warmth, competence sub-categories). This section links the *valence* of those spontaneous responses to populist attitudes from wave 1.

## Sample exclusions

```{r exclusions-participant}
# Analytic sample: returned to wave 2, completed the stereotype battery,
# and met minimum duration thresholds in both waves.
analytic_respondents <- waves_combined %>%
  filter(return_wave2 == 1) %>%
  filter(completed_stereotypes_wave2 == 1) %>%
  filter(duration_in_minutes    >= 3) %>%
  filter(duration_in_minutes_w2 >= 1)
```

## Response-level cleaning and validation

```{r exclusions-response}
# Pattern-based screen for low-information responses (blank, placeholder,
# pure-digit, "don't know" / "nothing" variants).
invalid_patterns <- c(
  "^\\s*$",                              # empty / whitespace
  "^(-+|\\.+|\\?+|/+)$",                 # placeholders
  "^\\d+$",                              # pure-digit answers (e.g. "1", "2", "3", "42")
  "^(lo yode[aa]|lo yod[ea]'a|lo yodea|dk|don'?t know|nothing|none)$"
)
invalid_regex <- paste(invalid_patterns, collapse = "|")

responses <- responses_direction_wave2 %>%
  semi_join(analytic_respondents, by = "pid") %>%
  filter(!is.na(response_direction)) %>%
  mutate(
    response_clean = str_squish(response),
    auto_invalid   = str_detect(tolower(response_clean), invalid_regex)
  )

# If the manually-coded `response_valid` flag has already been merged in,
# coalesce it with the automatic screen. Otherwise, use the automatic screen
# on its own.
if ("response_valid" %in% names(responses)) {
  responses <- responses %>%
    mutate(response_valid = dplyr::coalesce(response_valid, !auto_invalid))
} else {
  responses <- responses %>%
    mutate(response_valid = !auto_invalid)
}

responses <- responses %>%
  filter(response_valid) %>%
  mutate(
    pro_netanyahu_coalition = factor(
      pro_netanyahu_coalition,
      levels = c("Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Netanyahu coalition")
    ),
    civil_servant_label = factor(
      civil_servant_label,
      levels = c("public service employee",
                 "public service bureaucrat",
                 "public service clerk")
    ),
    valence     = as.numeric(response_direction),
    valence_ord = factor(response_direction, ordered = TRUE),
    valence_neg = as.integer(response_direction <= 2)
  )

nrow(responses)
```

## Merge content coding

```{r}
# Pull in the coded responses (warmth / competence sub-categories etc.).
coded <- readr::read_csv("coded_responses.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::select(
    response_id, participant_response_id, response_key,
    assertiveness, ability, sociability, morality,
    status, beliefs, other, erase,
    warmth, competence, source
  )

responses <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_key = stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_squish(response))
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(coded,
                   by = c("response_id", "participant_response_id", "response_key"))
```

## Distribution of response direction by experimental label

```{r}
# Cross-tab of response direction × content codes, by experimental label.
responses %>% labelled::remove_labels() %>%
  dplyr::select(response_direction, civil_servant_label, assertiveness:competence) %>%
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = civil_servant_label) %>%
  gtsummary::modify_caption("**Response direction and content codes by civil-servant label**")
```

```{r}

responses |>
  ggplot(aes(x = response_direction)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "grey35") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(after_stat(count) / sum(after_stat(count)),
                                        accuracy = 0.1)),
            stat = "count",
            vjust = -0.4,
            size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of response direction",
       x = "Response direction (1=Negative, 3=Neutral, 5=Positive)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_classic()

# Bar plot of response direction by experimental label.
responses |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_direction)) |>
  dplyr::count(civil_servant_label, response_direction) |>
  dplyr::group_by(civil_servant_label) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = response_direction, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "grey35") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 4) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.10))) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of response direction by experimental label",
       x = "Response direction (1 = Most negative … 5 = Most positive)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# פלטה: אדום=שלילי → אפור=ניטרלי → טורקיז=חיובי
valence_colors <- c(
  "1" = "#C44536",   # most negative
  "2" = "#E08D7A",
  "3" = "#B5B5B5",   # neutral
  "4" = "#7FB8C7",
  "5" = "#2E86AB"    # most positive
)

responses %>%
  filter(!is.na(response_direction)) %>%
  count(civil_servant_label, response_direction) %>%
  group_by(civil_servant_label) %>%
  mutate(
    pct = n / sum(n),
    N_total = sum(n)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    response_direction = factor(response_direction),
    facet_label = paste0(civil_servant_label, "\n(n = ", scales::comma(N_total), ")"),
    # סדר את ה-facets כך ש-bureaucrat (הסיפור) יהיה במרכז
    facet_label = forcats::fct_relevel(facet_label,
      paste0("public service employee\n(n = ",
             scales::comma(unique(N_total[civil_servant_label == "public service employee"])), ")"),
      paste0("public service clerk\n(n = ",
             scales::comma(unique(N_total[civil_servant_label == "public service clerk"])), ")"),
      paste0("public service bureaucrat\n(n = ",
             scales::comma(unique(N_total[civil_servant_label == "public service bureaucrat"])), ")")
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = response_direction, y = pct, fill = response_direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.8,
            color = "#1F3343", fontface = "bold",
            family = "Open Sans") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(facet_label)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = valence_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)),
    limits = c(0, 0.60)
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "1" = "1\nMost\nnegative",
    "2" = "2",
    "3" = "3\nNeutral",
    "4" = "4",
    "5" = "5\nMost\npositive"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Response direction by civil-servant label",
    subtitle = "Wave 2, Israel (Feb 2026). Color encodes valence (red = negative, blue = positive).",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of responses"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14, base_family = "Open Sans") +
  theme(
    panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#1F3343"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "#F0F2F5", color = NA),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      color = "#1F3343",
                                      lineheight = 0.9,
                                      margin = margin(6, 4, 6, 4)),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10, lineheight = 0.9, color = "#1F3343"),
    panel.spacing      = unit(1.4, "lines")
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)

# Pew-style palette: red → gray → blue
pew_colors <- c(
  "1" = "#7C2D3B",
  "2" = "#D27A6C",
  "3" = "#BFBFBF",
  "4" = "#7DAFC4",
  "5" = "#1F4E79"
)

pew_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Most negative",
  "2" = "Negative",
  "3" = "Neutral",
  "4" = "Positive",
  "5" = "Most positive"
)

position_data <- responses %>%
  filter(!is.na(response_direction)) %>%
  count(civil_servant_label, response_direction) %>%
  group_by(civil_servant_label) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n), n_total = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(response_direction = factor(response_direction,
                                     levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5"))) %>%
  arrange(civil_servant_label, response_direction) %>%
  group_by(civil_servant_label) %>%
  mutate(
    cum_end  = cumsum(pct),
    neg_size = sum(pct[response_direction %in% c("1", "2")]) +
               sum(pct[response_direction == "3"]) / 2,
    xmax = cum_end - neg_size,
    xmin = xmax - pct
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    civil_servant_label = fct_relevel(
      civil_servant_label,
      "public service employee",
      "public service clerk",
      "public service bureaucrat"
    ),
    label_y = paste0(civil_servant_label, "\n(n = ", comma(n_total), ")"),
    label_y = factor(label_y,
                     levels = rev(unique(label_y[order(civil_servant_label)])))
  )

side <- position_data %>%
  group_by(label_y) %>%
  summarise(
    neg = sum(pct[response_direction %in% c("1", "2")]),
    pos = sum(pct[response_direction %in% c("4", "5")]),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(position_data, aes(y = label_y)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = xmin, xend = xmax,
                   y = label_y, yend = label_y,
                   color = response_direction),
               linewidth = 28, lineend = "butt") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2,
                label = ifelse(pct >= 0.06,
                               percent(pct, accuracy = 1), "")),
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 4.3,
            family = "Open Sans") +
  geom_text(data = side,
            aes(x = -0.82, y = label_y,
                label = paste0(percent(neg, accuracy = 1), " NEG")),
            color = "#7C2D3B", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5,
            family = "Open Sans", hjust = 1) +
  geom_text(data = side,
            aes(x = 0.82, y = label_y,
                label = paste0("POS ", percent(pos, accuracy = 1))),
            color = "#1F4E79", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5,
            family = "Open Sans", hjust = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pew_colors, labels = pew_labels, name = NULL,
                     guide = guide_legend(nrow = 1,
                                          override.aes = list(linewidth = 7))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.6, 0.6, 0.2),
    labels = function(x) percent(abs(x), accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.95, 0.95), clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title    = "'Bureaucrat' label triggers the strongest negative reactions",
    subtitle = "Distribution of response valence by civil-servant label assigned to respondents.",
    caption  = "Wave 2, Israel (Feb 2026). Source: Alon-Barkat & Gilboa.",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14, base_family = "Open Sans") +
  theme(
    panel.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background      = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid           = element_blank(),
    plot.title           = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#1F3343",
                                         size = 18, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle        = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey45",
                                         margin = margin(b = 14)),
    plot.caption         = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey55",
                                         hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 12)),
    legend.position      = "top",
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    legend.box.spacing   = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    legend.text          = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y          = element_text(size = 12, color = "#1F3343",
                                         hjust = 1, lineheight = 0.9),
    axis.text.x          = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey45"),
    plot.margin          = margin(15, 25, 15, 15)
  )
```

## Most-frequent words by content code




```{r}
responses |> 
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(n = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(n)) |> 
  slice_head(n = 20) |> 
  gt::gt()


responses |>
  dplyr::group_by(response) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n          = dplyr::n(),
    warmth     = mean(warmth == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    competence = mean(competence == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dimension = dplyr::case_when(
    warmth & competence  ~ "Both",
    warmth               ~ "Warmth",
    competence           ~ "Competence",
    TRUE                 ~ "Other"
  )) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n)) |>
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 20) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Response   = response,
    Dimension  = factor(dimension, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Both", "Other")),
    Frequency  = n
  ) |>
  gt::gt() |>
  gt::tab_header(title = "Top 20 most frequent responses by content dimension")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr); library(gt)

top_20 <- responses |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_direction)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(response) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    warmth_p = mean(warmth == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    compet_p = mean(competence == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    mean_dir = mean(response_direction, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_1      = sum(response_direction == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_2      = sum(response_direction == 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_3      = sum(response_direction == 3, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_4      = sum(response_direction == 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_5      = sum(response_direction == 5, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dimension = dplyr::case_when(
    warmth_p & compet_p ~ "Both",
    warmth_p            ~ "Warmth",
    compet_p            ~ "Competence",
    TRUE                ~ "Other"
  )) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n)) |>
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 20) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Response         = response,
    Dimension        = factor(dimension, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Both", "Other")),
    Frequency        = n,
    `Mean direction` = round(mean_dir, 2),
    `1` = n_1, `2` = n_2, `3` = n_3, `4` = n_4, `5` = n_5
  )

top_20 |>
  gt::gt() |>
  gt::tab_header(
    title    = gt::md("**Top 20 most frequent responses**"),
    subtitle = gt::md("Coded by content dimension; valence distribution per response (1 = most negative, 5 = most positive)")
  ) |>
  gt::tab_spanner(
    label   = "Direction frequency",
    columns = c(`1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`)
  ) |>
  gt::data_color(
    columns = Dimension,
    method  = "factor",
    palette = c("Warmth"     = "#E07B91",
                "Competence" = "#4C9EBE",
                "Both"       = "#7E57C2",
                "Other"      = "grey85")
  ) |>
  gt::data_color(
    columns = `Mean direction`,
    method  = "numeric",
    palette = c("#F08080", "#F0D87B", "#3F88B5"),  # red → gold → blue, matching your bar plot
    domain  = c(1, 5)
  ) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "right",
                 columns = c(Frequency, `Mean direction`,
                             `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`)) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "left",
                 columns = c(Response, Dimension))
```


```{r}
library(dplyr); library(gt); library(purrr)

# ---- 1. Build top-20 with per-direction counts ----
top_20 <- responses |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_direction)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(response) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    warmth_p = mean(warmth == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    compet_p = mean(competence == 1, na.rm = TRUE) >= 0.5,
    mean_dir = mean(response_direction, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_1      = sum(response_direction == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_2      = sum(response_direction == 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_3      = sum(response_direction == 3, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_4      = sum(response_direction == 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_5      = sum(response_direction == 5, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dimension = dplyr::case_when(
    warmth_p & compet_p ~ "Both",
    warmth_p            ~ "Warmth",
    compet_p            ~ "Competence",
    TRUE                ~ "Other"
  )) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n)) |>
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 20)

# ---- 2. Helper: build inline SVG histogram for each row ----
# Bars are scaled to a *per-row* max so the shape of each row's distribution
# is visible regardless of the response's overall frequency. If you'd rather
# scale all rows to a shared max (so taller bars = more common response),
# replace `local_max` below with the global `max_count`.
make_bars <- function(counts, width = 90, height = 24, fill = "#1f3552") {
  counts    <- ifelse(is.na(counts), 0, counts)
  local_max <- max(counts, 1)
  bw        <- (width / 5) - 4
  bars <- vapply(seq_along(counts), function(i) {
    h <- counts[i] / local_max * (height - 2)
    x <- (i - 1) * (width / 5) + 2
    sprintf('<rect x="%g" y="%g" width="%g" height="%g" fill="%s"/>',
            x, height - h, bw, h, fill)
  }, character(1))
  paste0(
    sprintf('<svg width="%d" height="%d" style="display:block;">', width, height),
    paste0(bars, collapse = ""),
    '</svg>'
  )
}

top_20 <- top_20 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dist_svg = purrr::pmap_chr(
      list(n_1, n_2, n_3, n_4, n_5),
      ~ make_bars(c(..1, ..2, ..3, ..4, ..5))
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Response                 = response,
    Dimension                = factor(dimension, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Both", "Other")),
    Frequency                = n,
    `Mean direction`         = round(mean_dir, 2),
    `Direction distribution` = dist_svg
  )

# ---- 3. Render gt table ----
top_20 |>
  gt::gt() |>
  gt::fmt_markdown(columns = `Direction distribution`) |>
  gt::tab_header(
    title    = gt::md("**Top 20 most frequent responses**"),
    subtitle = "Coded by content dimension; bars show frequency at each direction value (1 \u2192 5, left to right)"
  ) |>
  gt::data_color(
    columns = Dimension,
    method  = "factor",
    palette = c("Warmth"     = "#E07B91",
                "Competence" = "#4C9EBE",
                "Both"       = "#7E57C2",
                "Other"      = "grey85")
  ) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "right",
                 columns = c(Frequency, `Mean direction`)) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "left",
                 columns = c(Response, Dimension)) |>
  gt::cols_align(align = "center",
                 columns = `Direction distribution`)
```

```{r}
# Top responses tagged warmth / morality / competence.
responses |> filter(warmth == 1) |>
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(n = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(n))

responses |> filter(morality == 1) |>
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(n = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(n))

responses |> filter(competence == 1) |>
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(n = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(n))
```

## Common traits by civil-servant label — valence × dimension

For each experimental label, the most frequently mentioned traits are placed
along a negative-to-positive valence continuum (mean `response_direction`).
Text size in the plot is proportional to how often the trait was mentioned;
color marks the content dimension (warmth / competence / both / other).

```{r common-traits-build}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# 1. English translations -------------------------------------------------
# Two-tier translation lookup. Tier (a) pulls the per-response `translation`
# column from `coded_responses_train.csv`. Tier (b) is a manual override for
# frequent Hebrew responses that were missing from (a) or that needed a
# clearer English gloss. Manual overrides always win.

translations_csv <- readr::read_csv("coded_responses_train.csv",
                                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::transmute(response,
                   response_en = translation) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_en),
                response_en != "NA",
                stringr::str_squish(response_en) != "") |>
  dplyr::count(response, response_en, sort = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::group_by(response) |>
  dplyr::slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(response, response_en)

# Manual overrides — extend this list whenever an untranslated Hebrew term
# shows up in the table or plot.
translations_manual <- tibble::tribble(
  ~response,        ~response_en,
  "תנאים",           "(work) conditions",
  "תנאים טובים",      "good (work) conditions",
  "סחבת",            "foot-dragging",
  "מסורבל",          "cumbersome",
  "טרחנים",          "fussy",
  "עייפות",          "tiredness",
  "נחמדים",          "nice",
  "שירות",           "service",
  "לא יודע",         "don't know",
  "לא יודעת",        "don't know"
)

translations <- translations_csv |>
  dplyr::anti_join(translations_manual, by = "response") |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(translations_manual)

# 2. Trait-level summary --------------------------------------------------
# Translate first, then group by the English trait so that morphological
# variants of the same word (e.g. אדיבים / אדיבות → "courteous") collapse
# into a single row. The original Hebrew variants are preserved as a
# comma-separated list. Dimension is the modal (most-frequent) content
# code across the underlying response-level rows.

mode_value <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  tab <- table(x)
  names(tab)[which.max(tab)]
}

trait_summary <- responses %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = dplyr::case_when(
      warmth == 1 & competence == 1 ~ "Both",
      warmth == 1                   ~ "Warmth",
      competence == 1               ~ "Competence",
      TRUE                          ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(translations, by = "response") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(response_en = dplyr::coalesce(response_en, response)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(civil_servant_label, response_en) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n              = dplyr::n(),
    mean_direction = mean(response_direction, na.rm = TRUE),
    dimension      = mode_value(dimension),
    responses_he   = paste(sort(unique(response)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = factor(dimension,
                       levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Both", "Other"))
  )

# 3. Keep the top-N traits per label for readable plot/table output -------
top_n_traits <- 20

top_traits <- trait_summary %>%
  dplyr::group_by(civil_servant_label) %>%
  dplyr::slice_max(n, n = top_n_traits, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()
```

### Table — most common traits by label

```{r common-traits-table}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

top_traits %>%
  dplyr::arrange(civil_servant_label, dplyr::desc(n)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Civil-servant label` = civil_servant_label,
    `Trait (English)`     = response_en,
    `Trait (Hebrew)`      = responses_he,
    Dimension             = dimension,
    Frequency             = n,
    `Mean valence`        = round(mean_direction, 2)
  ) %>%
  gt::gt(groupname_col = "Civil-servant label") %>%
  gt::tab_header(
    title    = gt::md("**Most common traits by civil-servant label**"),
    subtitle = gt::md(paste0("Top ", top_n_traits,
                             " traits per label, sorted by frequency.",
                             " *Mean valence* averages `response_direction`",
                             " (lower = more negative, higher = more positive)."))
  ) %>%
  gt::cols_align("left",   columns = c(`Trait (English)`, `Trait (Hebrew)`,
                                       Dimension)) %>%
  gt::cols_align("right",  columns = c(Frequency, `Mean valence`)) %>%
  gt::data_color(
    columns = `Mean valence`,
    palette = c("#B2182B", "#F4A582", "#F7F7F7", "#92C5DE", "#2166AC")
  )
```

### Plot — traits along the valence continuum

```{r common-traits-plot}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 9

# ggrepel keeps the trait labels from overlapping; text size encodes
# frequency, x-position encodes mean valence (negative → positive).

set.seed(42)  # ggrepel uses a randomised layout

ggplot(top_traits,
       aes(x = mean_direction,
           y = 0,
           label = response_en,
           size  = n,
           color = dimension)) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    max.overlaps      = Inf,
    box.padding       = 0.45,
    point.padding     = 0.05,
    segment.color     = NA,
    force             = 2,
    seed              = 42
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(civil_servant_label), switch = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "Mean response direction (negative \u2192 positive)",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.05))
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(name  = "Frequency",
                        range = c(3, 8)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Content dimension",
    values = c("Warmth"     = "#E07B91",
               "Competence" = "#4C9EBE",
               "Both"       = "#7E57C2",
               "Other"      = "grey55"),
    drop   = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Most common traits by civil-servant label",
    subtitle = paste0("Top ", top_n_traits,
                      " traits per label arranged along the valence continuum;",
                      " text size = frequency."),
    caption  = "Source: Wave 2 open-ended trait responses."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y      = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    strip.placement   = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    legend.position   = "bottom"
  ) +
  guides(
    size  = guide_legend(override.aes = list(label = "abc")),
    color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(label = "abc", size = 5))
  )
```

## Correlations: response direction × populism × trust × conspiracy × elite-perception

```{r}
# Pooled
responses %>% labelled::remove_labels() %>%
  dplyr::select(response_direction, populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                trust_civil_service, conspiracy_vaccine_n, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                  na.deletion = "pairwise",  
                   title = "All respondents")


# Netanyahu-coalition supporters
responses %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") %>%
  labelled::remove_labels() %>%
  dplyr::select(response_direction, populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                popular_sovereignty:manichean,
                trust_civil_service, conspiracy_vaccine_n, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                  na.deletion = "pairwise",  
                   title = "Netanyahu coalition supporters")

# Anti-Netanyahu / Other
responses %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") %>%
  labelled::remove_labels() %>%
  dplyr::select(response_direction, populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                popular_sovereignty:manichean,
                trust_civil_service, conspiracy_vaccine_n, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                  na.deletion = "pairwise",  
                   title = "Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters")
```


## Response direction ~ populist attitudes (4-dim index)

### Scatter plots

```{r}
# Pooled, colored by partisanship
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes index — pooled",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()

# Faceted by experimental label
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — by experimental label",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

# Faceted by experimental label, colored by partisanship
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — label × partisanship",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Multilevel models — full build-up

```{r}
# Random intercept on pid (each participant produced multiple responses).
# Models 1-2: baseline + interaction.
# Models 3-4: + demographics + SES + public-sector experience.
# Models 5-6: + ideology.
# Models 7-8: + polarization + external efficacy.
tmod0 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ 1 + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())

tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())

tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod0,tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction by populist attitudes (M1)")

sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × populism × partisanship (M2)")
```

```{r}
# Average slopes of populism, separately within each coalition camp (from M4).
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```

### Split-sample vs. fully-interacted cross-check

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# ---- 1. Split-sample approach ---------------------------------------------
f_base <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim +
  gender + age_group + education + income +
  subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
  (1 | pid)

dat_net  <- responses_direction_wave2 |>
  dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")
dat_anti <- responses_direction_wave2 |>
  dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")

m_net  <- lmerTest::lmer(f_base, data = dat_net,  REML = TRUE)
m_anti <- lmerTest::lmer(f_base, data = dat_anti, REML = TRUE)

# Populism slopes in each subgroup
b_net  <- lme4::fixef(m_net) ["populist_attitudes_index_4dim"]
b_anti <- lme4::fixef(m_anti)["populist_attitudes_index_4dim"]
se_net  <- sqrt(vcov(m_net) ["populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
                             "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"])
se_anti <- sqrt(vcov(m_anti)["populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
                             "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"])

# Test of equality: Wald Z on the difference.
# Subgroups are independent participants, so variances add.
b_diff  <- b_net - b_anti
se_diff <- sqrt(se_net^2 + se_anti^2)
z_diff  <- b_diff / se_diff
p_diff  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(z_diff))   # two-sided

split_table <- tibble::tibble(
  group = c("Netanyahu coalition",
            "Anti-Netanyahu / Other",
            "Difference (Net − Anti)"),
  beta  = c(b_net, b_anti, b_diff),
  se    = c(se_net, se_anti, se_diff),
  z     = c(NA_real_, NA_real_, z_diff),
  p_two_sided = c(NA_real_, NA_real_, p_diff)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(c(beta, se, z, p_two_sided), ~ round(.x, 3)))

print(split_table)


# ---- 2. Fully-interacted model (cross-check) ------------------------------
# Algebraically equivalent to split-sample: coalition interacted with
# populism AND with every demographic control. The populism × coalition
# coefficient is the exact counterpart of the split-sample difference.
f_full <- response_direction ~
  populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:gender +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:age_group +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:education +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:income +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:subjective_social_economic_status +
  pro_netanyahu_coalition:public_sector_experience +
  gender + age_group + education + income +
  subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
  (1 | pid)

m_full <- lmerTest::lmer(f_full, data = responses_direction_wave2, REML = TRUE)

# Extract the focal interaction
broom.mixed::tidy(m_full, effects = "fixed") |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term,
    "populist_attitudes_index_4dim:pro_netanyahu_coalition")) |>
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value)

# Side-by-side presentation
sjPlot::tab_model(m_anti, m_net, m_full,
                  dv.labels = c("Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                                "Netanyahu coalition",
                                "Fully interacted"),
                  show.ci = FALSE, show.se = TRUE, p.style = "numeric")
```

### Cross-check on cleaned-response sample

```{r}
# Same M1/M2 logic, but using `responses` (post-cleaning) rather than the raw
# wave-2 response data. Provides a sanity check that exclusions don't change conclusions.
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses)
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)
summary(tmod1)
summary(tmod2)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, dv.labels = "M1: cleaned-response sample")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction (cleaned sample, M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × populism × partisanship (cleaned sample, M2)")
```

```{r}
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod2,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```


## Response direction ~ anti-elitism (sub-dimension)

Same models, but using only the anti-elitism sub-dimension as the populism predictor — theoretically the dimension most directly tied to evaluations of public officials.

### Scatter plots

```{r}
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — pooled",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — by experimental label",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — label × partisanship",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Multilevel models — full build-up

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred", terms = "anti_elitism [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction by anti-elitism (M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("anti_elitism", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × anti-elitism × partisanship (M2)")
```

```{r}
# NOTE: original code requested slopes on `populist_attitudes_index_4dim` here,
# but that variable is not in M4 (which uses anti_elitism). Slopes requested on anti_elitism.
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "anti_elitism",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```


## Subset analyses — warmth-coded responses

Restricting to responses that the manual coders flagged as belonging to the **warmth** content category. This isolates whether populism predicts more negative valence specifically about civil servants' personality / sociability / morality.

### Anti-elitism, warmth subset

```{r}
responses %>% filter(warmth == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — warmth subset, by label",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(warmth == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — warmth subset, label × partisanship",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels() |> filter(warmth == 1))
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

### Populism (4-dim), warmth subset

```{r}
responses %>% filter(warmth == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — warmth subset, pooled",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(warmth == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — warmth subset, by label",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(warmth == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — warmth subset, label × partisanship",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(warmth == 1) |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction (warmth subset, M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × populism × partisanship (warmth subset, M2)")
```

```{r}
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```


## Subset analyses — competence-coded responses

```{r}
# Most-frequent competence-coded responses + spot-check of high-populism extremes.
responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  group_by(response) |>
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean_direction = mean(response_direction)) |>
  arrange(desc(n))

# Spot-check: respondents at the high end of populism — what did they write?
responses |> filter(populist_attitudes_index_4dim == 1) |>
  dplyr::select(pid, civil_servant_label, response, response_direction,
                pro_netanyahu_coalition, warmth, competence) |> view()
```

### Populism (4-dim), competence subset

```{r}
responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — competence subset, pooled",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — competence subset, by label",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by populist attitudes — competence subset, label × partisanship",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(competence == 1) |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction (competence subset, M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × populism × partisanship (competence subset, M2)")
```

```{r}
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```

### Anti-elitism, competence subset

```{r}
responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — competence subset, by label",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>% filter(competence == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = anti_elitism, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by anti-elitism — competence subset, label × partisanship",
       x = "Anti-elitism",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels() |> filter(competence == 1))
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```


## Subset analysis — bureaucrat-label only

Restricts to respondents randomly assigned the *bureaucrat* label, which is theoretically the most loaded of the three.

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels() |>
                              filter(civil_servant_label == "public service bureaucrat"))
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim [all]") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction (bureaucrat label only, M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × populism × partisanship (bureaucrat label, M2)")
```

```{r}
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```


# Alternative populism index — multiplicative

NOTE: the original section header was "Minimum (Huttke 2020)", but the code below uses the **multiplicative** index (`populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication`), not the minimum operator. Renamed for accuracy. ("Wuttke 2020" rather than "Huttke" if the citation referred to Wuttke et al. 2020.)

```{r}
# Scatter plots of response direction by the multiplicative populism index.
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by multiplicative populism index — pooled",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim, multiplicative)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by multiplicative populism index — by label",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim, multiplicative)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by multiplicative populism index — label × partisanship",
       x = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim, multiplicative)",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
tmod6 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)
tmod8 <- tmod7 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5, tmod6, tmod7, tmod8,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics", "M4: M3 + interaction",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M6: M5 + interaction",
                                "M7: + polarization & efficacy", "M8: M7 + interaction"))
```

```{r}
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod1, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication") +
  labs(title = "Predicted response direction (multiplicative index, M1)")
sjPlot::plot_model(tmod2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Response direction × multiplicative populism × partisanship (M2)")
```

```{r}
simple_slopes_h3a <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  tmod4,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
simple_slopes_h3a
```


# Dimension-by-dimension models

Splitting the 4 sub-dimensions of populism (anti-elitism, popular sovereignty, homogeneity, Manichean) so we can see which dimension(s) drive any overall populism effect on response direction. NOTE: the original formulas listed `anti_elitism` twice — duplicate removed below; lme4 would silently drop the duplicate but the formula is now correct.

## Netanyahu-coalition supporters

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + popular_sovereignty + homogeneity + manichean + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod3, tmod5, tmod7,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Netanyahu coalition supporters — dimension models",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: dimensions", "M3: + demographics",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M7: + polarization & efficacy"))
```

## Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + popular_sovereignty + homogeneity + manichean + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod3, tmod5, tmod7,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters — dimension models",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: dimensions", "M3: + demographics",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M7: + polarization & efficacy"))
```

## Netanyahu-coalition supporters, warmth subset

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + popular_sovereignty + homogeneity + manichean + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              filter(warmth == 1) |> labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                              subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience)

tmod5 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)

tmod7 <- tmod5 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod3, tmod5, tmod7,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Netanyahu coalition — warmth subset, dimension models",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: dimensions", "M3: + demographics",
                                "M5: + ideology", "M7: + polarization & efficacy"))
```


# Civil servants as an "elite group"

Two distinct elite-perception measures:
- `elite_group_civil_servants` — wave-1 binary (do you perceive civil servants as part of an elite group?)
- `civil_servant_elite_wave2` — wave-2 continuous (extent to which civil servants are seen as an elite group).

We first check whether each elite-perception measure adds explanatory power on top of the populism dimensions, then compare the two measures side-by-side.

## Wave-1 elite-group measure (binary)

### Netanyahu-coalition supporters

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + popular_sovereignty + homogeneity + manichean + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod2 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ elite_group_civil_servants + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + elite_group_civil_servants)

tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + elite_group_civil_servants * anti_elitism)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Netanyahu coalition — populism + wave-1 elite group",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: dimensions only",
                                "M2: elite-group only",
                                "M3: + elite-group",
                                "M4: + elite-group × anti-elitism"))
```

### Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + popular_sovereignty + homogeneity + manichean + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod2 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ elite_group_civil_servants + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + elite_group_civil_servants)

tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + elite_group_civil_servants * anti_elitism)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Anti-Netanyahu / Other — populism + wave-1 elite group",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: dimensions only",
                                "M2: elite-group only",
                                "M3: + elite-group",
                                "M4: + elite-group × anti-elitism"))
```

## Wave-2 elite-perception measure (continuous)

### Cross-tab: wave-2 (continuous) × wave-1 (binary)

```{r}
responses |>
  ggplot(aes(x = civil_servant_elite_wave2)) +
  geom_bar() +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(elite_group_civil_servants)) +
  labs(title = "Wave-2 elite-perception by wave-1 elite-group flag",
```
