```{r}
       x = "Wave-2 elite-perception score",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Netanyahu-coalition supporters — wave-2 elite vs. anti-elitism

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod2 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ civil_servant_elite_wave2 + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") |>
                              labelled::remove_labels())

tmod3 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + civil_servant_elite_wave2)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Netanyahu coalition — anti-elitism vs. wave-2 elite-perception",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: anti-elitism only",
                                "M2: wave-2 elite only",
                                "M3: + wave-2 elite"))
```

### Pooled — wave-2 elite × partisanship

```{r}
tmod1 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ anti_elitism + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod2 <- tmod1 |> update(. ~ . + anti_elitism * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod3 <- lme4::lmer(response_direction ~ civil_servant_elite_wave2 + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid),
                    data = responses |> labelled::remove_labels())
tmod4 <- tmod3 |> update(. ~ . + civil_servant_elite_wave2 * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

tmod5 <- tmod2 |> update(. ~ . + civil_servant_elite_wave2 * pro_netanyahu_coalition)

sjPlot::tab_model(tmod1, tmod2, tmod3, tmod4, tmod5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  dv.labels = c("M1: anti-elitism + camp",
                                "M2: + anti-elitism × camp",
                                "M3: wave-2 elite + camp",
                                "M4: + wave-2 elite × camp",
                                "M5: anti-elitism × camp + wave-2 elite × camp"))
```

### Scatter plots — wave-2 elite-perception

```{r}
responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = civil_servant_elite_wave2, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by wave-2 elite-perception — pooled",
       x = "Wave-2 elite-perception of civil servants",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = civil_servant_elite_wave2, y = response_direction)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Response direction by wave-2 elite-perception — by label",
       x = "Wave-2 elite-perception of civil servants",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()

responses %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = civil_servant_elite_wave2, y = response_direction,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Response direction by wave-2 elite-perception — label × partisanship",
       x = "Wave-2 elite-perception of civil servants",
       y = "Response direction (1=Negative, 2=Neutral, 3=Positive)",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(civil_servant_label)) +
  theme_minimal()
```


# Scale-based stereotype measures — secondary analyses

Per the pre-registration, the SSCM-based response-level measure
(`response_direction`) is the **primary** outcome and carries all
preregistered hypothesis tests (H1–H3b). This section reports the
**secondary, complementary** analyses on the closed-scale stereotype
items, used here for *robustness, validation, and descriptive purposes*.

The closed scales include eight bipolar trait items (`friendly`,
`sociable`, `trustworthy`, `fair`, `competent`, `skilled`, `confident`,
`assertive`, `goal_oriented`) and a global image rating
(`general_image_personal`, `general_image_social`). They map onto:

- **Warmth** = mean(friendly, sociable, trustworthy, fair)
- **Competence** = mean(competent, skilled, confident, assertive, goal_oriented)
- **General image** = mean(general_image_personal, general_image_social)

We mirror the SSCM analyses by running, for each composite, (i) bivariate
correlations and (ii) regressions parallel to H1, H2, H3a, H3b. We add
exploratory tests with **competence** as outcome (not pre-registered) and
dimension-by-dimension models that decompose the populism index.

## Build composite scale indices

```{r scales-build}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Respondent-level data frame: combined-waves data with labels removed,
# restricted to the analytic respondent sample.
scales_df <- waves_combined %>%
  labelled::remove_labels() %>%
  semi_join(analytic_respondents, by = "pid") %>%
  mutate(
    scale_warmth     = rowMeans(across(c(friendly_scale, sociable_scale,
                                         trustworthy_scale, fair_scale)),
                                na.rm = TRUE),
    scale_competence = rowMeans(across(c(competent_scale, skilled_scale,
                                         confident_scale, assertive_scale,
                                         goal_oriented_scale)),
                                na.rm = TRUE),
    scale_general    = rowMeans(across(c(general_image_personal,
                                         general_image_social)),
                                na.rm = TRUE),
    # 4-facet composites matching the best-fitting CFA model
    # (Sociability / Morality / Ability / Assertiveness). `confident_scale`
    # is NOT included here because it was not in the CFA — it stays in the
    # broader `scale_competence` average.
    facet_sociability  = rowMeans(across(c(friendly_scale, sociable_scale)),
                                  na.rm = TRUE),
    facet_morality     = rowMeans(across(c(trustworthy_scale, fair_scale)),
                                  na.rm = TRUE),
    facet_ability      = rowMeans(across(c(competent_scale, skilled_scale)),
                                  na.rm = TRUE),
    facet_assertiveness = rowMeans(across(c(assertive_scale, goal_oriented_scale)),
                                   na.rm = TRUE),
    pro_netanyahu_coalition = factor(
      pro_netanyahu_coalition,
      levels = c("Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Netanyahu coalition")
    )
  )
```

## Reliability — Cronbach's alpha for composite scales

```{r scales-alpha}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Internal-consistency reliability for each composite. We use psych::alpha,
# which reports raw and standardized alpha plus item-removed diagnostics.
# Items are entered in their bipolar-scale form (already keyed so that higher
# = more positive evaluation).

alpha_warmth <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(friendly_scale, sociable_scale,
                              trustworthy_scale, fair_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)

alpha_competence <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(competent_scale, skilled_scale,
                              confident_scale, assertive_scale,
                              goal_oriented_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)

alpha_general <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(general_image_personal, general_image_social),
  warnings = FALSE
)

# 4-facet composites (CFA-aligned). Each has only 2 items, so alpha will be
# essentially the Spearman-Brown adjusted correlation between the items.
alpha_sociability <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(friendly_scale, sociable_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)
alpha_morality <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(trustworthy_scale, fair_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)
alpha_ability <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(competent_scale, skilled_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)
alpha_assertiveness <- psych::alpha(
  scales_df %>% dplyr::select(assertive_scale, goal_oriented_scale),
  warnings = FALSE
)

# Compact summary of raw + standardized alpha for all composites.
tibble::tibble(
  Composite             = c("Warmth (4 items)",
                            "Competence (5 items)",
                            "General image (2 items)",
                            "Sociability facet (2 items)",
                            "Morality facet (2 items)",
                            "Ability facet (2 items)",
                            "Assertiveness facet (2 items)"),
  `Items`               = c("friendly, sociable, trustworthy, fair",
                            "competent, skilled, confident, assertive, goal_oriented",
                            "general_image_personal, general_image_social",
                            "friendly, sociable",
                            "trustworthy, fair",
                            "competent, skilled",
                            "assertive, goal_oriented"),
  `Raw alpha`           = c(alpha_warmth$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_competence$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_general$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_sociability$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_morality$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_ability$total$raw_alpha,
                            alpha_assertiveness$total$raw_alpha),
  `Std. alpha`          = c(alpha_warmth$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_competence$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_general$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_sociability$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_morality$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_ability$total$std.alpha,
                            alpha_assertiveness$total$std.alpha),
  `Avg. inter-item r`   = c(alpha_warmth$total$average_r,
                            alpha_competence$total$average_r,
                            alpha_general$total$average_r,
                            alpha_sociability$total$average_r,
                            alpha_morality$total$average_r,
                            alpha_ability$total$average_r,
                            alpha_assertiveness$total$average_r),
  `N`                   = c(nrow(alpha_warmth$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_competence$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_general$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_sociability$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_morality$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_ability$scores),
                            nrow(alpha_assertiveness$scores))
) %>%
  gt::gt() %>%
  gt::fmt_number(columns = c(`Raw alpha`, `Std. alpha`, `Avg. inter-item r`),
                 decimals = 3) %>%
  gt::tab_header(title = "Cronbach's alpha for composite stereotype scales",
                 subtitle = "Respondent-level analytic sample, labels removed")
```

```{r scales-alpha-items}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Item-level diagnostics: each item's correlation with the rest of the scale,
# and what alpha would become if that item were dropped. Useful for spotting
# items that drag reliability down (we expect 'assertive' and possibly
# 'goal_oriented' to be weakest within the competence composite, given the
# CFA showed lower loadings for those items on the agency/Assertiveness
# factor).

print_alpha_drop <- function(a, label) {
  a$alpha.drop %>%
    tibble::as_tibble(rownames = "Item") %>%
    dplyr::select(Item, raw_alpha, std.alpha, average_r) %>%
    gt::gt() %>%
    gt::fmt_number(columns = -Item, decimals = 3) %>%
    gt::tab_header(title = paste0("Alpha if item dropped — ", label))
}

print_alpha_drop(alpha_warmth,     "Warmth")
print_alpha_drop(alpha_competence, "Competence")
print_alpha_drop(alpha_general,    "General image")
```

Conventional benchmarks: α ≥ .70 acceptable, ≥ .80 good, ≥ .90 excellent.
The CFA in the wave-1-style closed-scale section above is the structural
counterpart of these reliabilities — items load on a 4-factor
Sociability/Morality/Ability/Assertiveness structure rather than cleanly on
a 2-factor warmth/competence structure, which is why the 5-item competence
composite is expected to be slightly less internally consistent than the
4-item warmth composite.

## Distributions of composite indices by label

```{r scales-dist}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>%
  dplyr::select(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence,
                civil_servant_label) %>%
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = civil_servant_label,
                         missing_text = "Missing") %>%
  gtsummary::modify_caption("**Composite stereotype scales by experimental label**") %>%
  gtsummary::bold_labels()

# Visual: density of each composite by label.
scales_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence),
                      names_to  = "scale",
                      values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = civil_servant_label)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ scale, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of composite stereotype scales by label",
       x = "Composite score", y = "Density",
       fill = "Civil-servant label") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Bivariate correlations

### Scale composites × populism, trust, and elite-perception

```{r scales-corr-pooled}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>%
  dplyr::select(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, trust_civil_service,
                civil_servant_elite_wave2,
                right_left_ideology, affective_polarization) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Correlations: stereotype scales × populism × trust × elite-perception (pooled)")
```

```{r scales-corr-by-camp}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") %>%
  dplyr::select(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Netanyahu-coalition supporters")

scales_df %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") %>%
  dplyr::select(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters")
```

### Validation — closed scales × SSCM-based mean valence

Each respondent's mean `response_direction` (across their open-ended
responses) should be positively correlated with the closed-scale
composites. A strong correlation supports the convergent validity of the
two stereotype measures.

```{r scales-corr-sscm}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Per-respondent mean valence from the SSCM responses.
respondent_valence <- responses %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pid) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_valence_sscm = mean(response_direction, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

scales_df %>%
  dplyr::left_join(respondent_valence, by = "pid") %>%
  dplyr::select(mean_valence_sscm,
                scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Convergent validity: SSCM mean valence × closed-scale composites")
```

### Scatter plots — populism × scale composites

```{r scales-scatter-populism}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 4.5

scales_long <- scales_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(scale_general, scale_warmth, scale_competence),
                      names_to  = "scale",
                      values_to = "value")

ggplot(scales_long,
       aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = value,
           color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.05, height = 0.05) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~ scale, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Closed-scale stereotypes by populist attitudes",
       x     = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y     = "Composite score",
       color = "Partisanship") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Pre-registered (secondary): H1 — general image ~ populism

Parallel to H1 on the SSCM measure: expectation is a *negative* main
effect of populist attitudes on overall stereotype favorability
(`scale_general` is reverse-keyed against negativity, so a negative slope
is consistent with the populism→negative-stereotype hypothesis).

```{r scales-h1}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_general ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "H1 (scale, secondary): general image ~ populism",
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + camp",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))

sjPlot::plot_model(m5, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim") +
  labs(title = "Predicted general-image score by populist attitudes (M5)")
```

## Pre-registered (secondary): H2 — warmth ~ populism

```{r scales-h2}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_warmth ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "H2 (scale, secondary): warmth ~ populism",
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + camp",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))

sjPlot::plot_model(m5, type = "pred",
                   terms = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim") +
  labs(title = "Predicted warmth score by populist attitudes (M5)")
```

## Pre-registered (secondary): H3a — general image × partisanship

```{r scales-h3a}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_general ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim:pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m2 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "H3a (scale, secondary): general image × partisanship",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: main effects", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))

sjPlot::plot_model(m2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "General image × populism × partisanship (M2)")

# Simple slopes by partisanship camp.
marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  m3,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
```

## Pre-registered (secondary): H3b — warmth × partisanship

```{r scales-h3b}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_warmth ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim:pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m2 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "H3b (scale, secondary): warmth × partisanship",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: main effects", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))

sjPlot::plot_model(m2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Warmth × populism × partisanship (M2)")

marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  m3,
  variables = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim",
  by = "pro_netanyahu_coalition"
)
```

## Exploratory: competence as outcome

Not pre-registered. Tested for completeness, since the closed scales also
yield a competence dimension; the SSCM evidence in this study suggests
the warmth pathway is the most likely locus of any populism effect, but
we report competence parallels for transparency.

### Main effect

```{r scales-comp-main}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_competence ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Exploratory (scale): competence ~ populism",
                  dv.labels = c("M1", "M2: + camp",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))
```

### Moderation by partisanship

```{r scales-comp-int}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

m1 <- lm(scale_competence ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition,
         data = scales_df)
m2 <- m1 |> update(. ~ . + populist_attitudes_index_4dim:pro_netanyahu_coalition)
m3 <- m2 |> update(. ~ . + gender + age_group + education + income +
                          subjective_social_economic_status +
                          public_sector_experience)
m4 <- m3 |> update(. ~ . + right_left_ideology)
m5 <- m4 |> update(. ~ . + affective_polarization + external_efficecy)

sjPlot::tab_model(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5,
                  show.se = TRUE, collapse.se = TRUE, show.std = TRUE,
                  title = "Exploratory (scale): competence × partisanship",
                  dv.labels = c("M1: main effects", "M2: + interaction",
                                "M3: + demographics",
                                "M4: + ideology",
                                "M5: + polarization & efficacy"))

sjPlot::plot_model(m2, type = "int",
                   terms = c("populist_attitudes_index_4dim", "pro_netanyahu_coalition")) +
  labs(title = "Competence × populism × partisanship (M2)")
```


## Facet-level analyses (CFA-aligned 4 dimensions)

The CFA above (Wave-1 closed-scale section) showed that the 8 trait items
are best described by **four** facets — Sociability, Morality, Ability,
Assertiveness — rather than by the 2-factor warmth/competence model. This
subsection mirrors the H1 / H3a regressions and the bivariate
correlations on each of these four facets, so that the populism →
stereotype relationship can be inspected at the facet level the data
actually support.

These are **exploratory** with respect to the pre-registration. Treat
them as descriptive evidence about *which* component of the closed-scale
evaluation moves with populism, not as confirmatory tests.

### Distributions of the 4 facets by label

```{r facets-dist}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>%
  dplyr::select(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                facet_ability, facet_assertiveness,
                civil_servant_label) %>%
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = civil_servant_label,
                         missing_text = "Missing") %>%
  gtsummary::modify_caption("**4-facet stereotype scales by experimental label**") %>%
  gtsummary::bold_labels()

scales_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                        facet_ability, facet_assertiveness),
                      names_to  = "facet",
                      values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = civil_servant_label)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ facet, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of 4-facet stereotype scales by label",
       x = "Facet score", y = "Density",
       fill = "Civil-servant label") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Bivariate correlations — pooled and by camp

```{r facets-corr-pooled}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>%
  dplyr::select(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                facet_ability, facet_assertiveness,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, trust_civil_service,
                civil_servant_elite_wave2,
                right_left_ideology, affective_polarization) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Correlations: 4 facets × populism × trust × elite-perception (pooled)")
```

```{r facets-corr-by-camp}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scales_df %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition") %>%
  dplyr::select(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                facet_ability, facet_assertiveness,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Netanyahu-coalition supporters")

scales_df %>% dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other") %>%
  dplyr::select(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                facet_ability, facet_assertiveness,
                populist_attitudes_index_4dim,
                anti_elitism, popular_sovereignty, homogeneity, manichean,
                trust_civil_service_wave2, civil_servant_elite_wave2) %>%
  sjPlot::tab_corr(triangle = "lower",
                   title = "Anti-Netanyahu / Other supporters")
```

### Scatter plots — populism × facets

```{r facets-scatter}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

scales_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(facet_sociability, facet_morality,
                        facet_ability, facet_assertiveness),
                      names_to  = "facet",
                      values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = populist_attitudes_index_4dim, y = value,
             color = pro_netanyahu_coalition)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.05, height = 0.05) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~ facet, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Closed-scale facets by populist attitudes",
       x     = "Populist attitudes index (4-dim)",
       y     = "Facet score",
       color = "Camp") +
  theme_minimal()
```



# Public-sector work information

Final breakdown by `public_service_work_information` (whether the participant reports actively seeking information about the public sector / public service careers).

```{r}
waves_combined_labs %>%
  dplyr::select(gender_label, age_group_label, religiosity_label, education_label, income_label,
                sector_employment_label, public_sector_employee_current,
                public_sector_experience, right_left_ideology, coalition_party_vote_intention,
                return_wave2, completed_wave2, completed_stereotypes_wave2,
                duration_in_minutes_w2, public_service_work_information) %>%
  tbl_summary(by = public_service_work_information,
    label = list(
      age_group_label ~ "Age Group",
      religiosity_label ~ "Religiosity",
      education_label ~ "Education",
      income_label ~ "Income (relative to average)",
      sector_employment_label ~ "Employment Sector",
      public_sector_employee_current ~ "Currently employed in public sector",
      public_sector_experience ~ "Any public sector experience",
      right_left_ideology ~ "Right-Left Ideology (1=extreme right, 7=extreme left)"
    ),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 9. Demographics by public-sector-work-information seeking**") %>%
  bold_labels()
```



# Coefficient plots for all models


```{r}

library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
sesoi   <- 0.15

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample (so subgroup betas share a denominator) ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
    # gender, public_sector_experience, pro_netanyahu_coalition kept as-is (binary; Gelman convention)
  )

# ---- 2. Formulas ----
f_basic_all <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_full_all  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

f_basic_sub <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + (1 | pid)
f_full_sub  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

f_int_basic <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_int_full  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

# ---- 3. Data subsets ----
dat_all  <- responses_std
dat_anti <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 4. Helpers (90% CIs to match pre-registered one-tailed alpha = .05) ----
fit_extract_main <- function(formula, data, group_label, spec_label) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = group_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_interaction <- function(formula, data, spec_label) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition", spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 5. Fit & extract ----
main_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_all, dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_all,  dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Main effect of populism")

int_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_basic, dat_all, "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_full,  dat_all, "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Interaction (full sample)")

plot_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(main_results, int_results) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    group = factor(group,
                   levels = c("Entire sample", "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                              "Netanyahu coalition", "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")),
    spec  = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Main effect of populism", "Interaction (full sample)"))
  )

# ---- 6. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
  "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 7. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = group,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec,
                    group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,    # filled
                                "With controls"    = 21)) + # open
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.25, 0.25)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" (90% CI) on response direction"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```





```{r}
library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr); library(purrr)

sesoi <- 0.15

all_dims <- c("popular_sovereignty", "anti_elitism", "homogeneity", "manichean")
ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                = z(response_direction),
    popular_sovereignty               = z(popular_sovereignty),
    anti_elitism                      = z(anti_elitism),
    homogeneity                       = z(homogeneity),
    manichean                         = z(manichean),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
  )

dat_all  <- responses_std
dat_anti <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 2. Formula builders ----
# Full sample (includes coalition main effect)
build_basic_all <- function(dim) {
  reformulate(c(dim, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", "(1 | pid)"),
              response = "response_direction")
}
build_full_all <- function(dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(dim, others, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
              response = "response_direction")
}

# Subgroup (no coalition; constant within subgroup)
build_basic_sub <- function(dim) {
  reformulate(c(dim, "(1 | pid)"), response = "response_direction")
}
build_full_sub <- function(dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(dim, others, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
              response = "response_direction")
}

# Interaction (focal dim x coalition; other dims kept additive)
build_int_basic <- function(dim) {
  reformulate(c(paste0(dim, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), "(1 | pid)"),
              response = "response_direction")
}
build_int_full <- function(dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(paste0(dim, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), others, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
              response = "response_direction")
}

# ---- 3. Helpers ----
fit_extract_main <- function(dim, data, panel_label, spec_label, build_formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(build_formula(dim), data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == dim) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, dimension = dim, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_int <- function(dim, data, spec_label, build_formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(build_formula(dim), data = data, REML = TRUE)
  pattern <- paste0("^", dim, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     dimension = dim, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 4. Fit & extract ----
results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  # Entire sample, both specs
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_all, panel_label = "Entire sample",
                 spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = build_basic_all),
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_all, panel_label = "Entire sample",
                 spec_label = "With controls", build_formula = build_full_all),
  # Anti-Netanyahu/Other
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_anti, panel_label = "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                 spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = build_basic_sub),
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_anti, panel_label = "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                 spec_label = "With controls", build_formula = build_full_sub),
  # Netanyahu coalition
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_net, panel_label = "Netanyahu coalition",
                 spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = build_basic_sub),
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_main,
                 data = dat_net, panel_label = "Netanyahu coalition",
                 spec_label = "With controls", build_formula = build_full_sub),
  # Interaction (full sample)
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_int,
                 data = dat_all, spec_label = "Without controls",
                 build_formula = build_int_basic),
  purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, fit_extract_int,
                 data = dat_all, spec_label = "With controls",
                 build_formula = build_int_full)
)

# ---- 5. Pretty labels & factor ordering ----
dim_labels <- c(popular_sovereignty = "Popular sovereignty",
                anti_elitism        = "Anti-elitism",
                homogeneity         = "Homogeneity",
                manichean           = "Manichean")

panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")

plot_df <- results |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = factor(dimension, levels = all_dims, labels = dim_labels[all_dims]),
    spec      = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel     = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
  )

# ---- 6. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "'With controls' models adjust for the other three populism dimensions plus:",
  "gender, age, education, income, subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  "Subgroup models drop coalition (constant within subgroup); interaction panel shows focal dimension \u00D7 coalition.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 7. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = dimension,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                "With controls"    = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" (90% CI) on response direction"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```



```{r}
library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
sesoi   <- 0.15

ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))
responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
  )

# ---- 2. Formulas (identical to the headline plot) ----
f_basic_all <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_full_all  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)
f_basic_sub <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + (1 | pid)
f_full_sub  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)
f_int_basic <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_int_full  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

# ---- 3. Subsets (response-level: warmth == 1 OR competence == 1) ----
dat_warmth      <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(warmth == 1)
dat_compet      <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(competence == 1)

dat_warmth_anti <- dat_warmth |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_warmth_net  <- dat_warmth |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")
dat_compet_anti <- dat_compet |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_compet_net  <- dat_compet |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 4. Helpers ----
fit_extract_main <- function(data, panel_label, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_int <- function(data, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  pattern <- paste0("^", pop_var, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 5. Fit & extract ----
results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  # Entire sample
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth, "Entire sample", "Warmth",     "Without controls", f_basic_all),
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth, "Entire sample", "Warmth",     "With controls",    f_full_all),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet, "Entire sample", "Competence", "Without controls", f_basic_all),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet, "Entire sample", "Competence", "With controls",    f_full_all),
  # Anti-Netanyahu/Other
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Warmth",     "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Warmth",     "With controls",    f_full_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Competence", "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", "Competence", "With controls",    f_full_sub),
  # Netanyahu coalition
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth_net, "Netanyahu coalition", "Warmth",     "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_warmth_net, "Netanyahu coalition", "Warmth",     "With controls",    f_full_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet_net, "Netanyahu coalition", "Competence", "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
  fit_extract_main(dat_compet_net, "Netanyahu coalition", "Competence", "With controls",    f_full_sub),
  # Interaction (full sample, but subsetted by content)
  fit_extract_int(dat_warmth, "Warmth",     "Without controls", f_int_basic),
  fit_extract_int(dat_warmth, "Warmth",     "With controls",    f_int_full),
  fit_extract_int(dat_compet, "Competence", "Without controls", f_int_basic),
  fit_extract_int(dat_compet, "Competence", "With controls",    f_int_full)
)

# ---- 6. Factor ordering ----
panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")

plot_df <- results |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence")),
    spec    = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel   = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
  )

# ---- 7. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "Each panel restricts to responses coded as warmth-related (warmth = 1) or competence-related (competence = 1).",
  "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
  "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 8. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = outcome,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                "With controls"    = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" of populism on response direction (90% CI)"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```




```{r}
library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
sesoi   <- 0.15

ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))
responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
  )

# ---- 2. Formulas (identical to the headline plot) ----
f_basic_all <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_full_all  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)
f_basic_sub <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + (1 | pid)
f_full_sub  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)
f_int_basic <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_int_full  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

# ---- 3. Subsets by civil servant label ----
labels_vec <- c("public service employee",
                "public service bureaucrat",
                "public service clerk")

# A list of subsets for each label x sample combination
make_label_subsets <- function(label) {
  list(
    all  = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label),
    anti = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label,
                                          pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other"),
    net  = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label,
                                          pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")
  )
}
subs <- stats::setNames(lapply(labels_vec, make_label_subsets), labels_vec)

# ---- 4. Helpers ----
fit_extract_main <- function(data, panel_label, label_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, label = label_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_int <- function(data, label_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  pattern <- paste0("^", pop_var, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     label = label_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 5. Fit & extract for all three labels ----
fit_one_label <- function(label) {
  s <- subs[[label]]
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    fit_extract_main(s$all,  "Entire sample",         label, "Without controls", f_basic_all),
    fit_extract_main(s$all,  "Entire sample",         label, "With controls",    f_full_all),
    fit_extract_main(s$anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
    fit_extract_main(s$anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  label, "With controls",    f_full_sub),
    fit_extract_main(s$net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
    fit_extract_main(s$net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   label, "With controls",    f_full_sub),
    fit_extract_int(s$all,                            label, "Without controls", f_int_basic),
    fit_extract_int(s$all,                            label, "With controls",    f_int_full)
  )
}

results <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(labels_vec, fit_one_label))

# ---- 6. Pretty labels & factor ordering ----
label_pretty <- c(
  "public service employee"   = "Public service employee",
  "public service bureaucrat" = "Public service bureaucrat",
  "public service clerk"      = "Public service clerk"
)

panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")

plot_df <- results |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = labels_vec, labels = label_pretty[labels_vec]),
    spec  = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
  )

# ---- 7. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "Each panel is fit separately within respondents assigned to each civil-servant label condition.",
  "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
  "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 8. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = label,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                "With controls"    = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" of populism on response direction (90% CI)"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```

First order

```{r}
library(broom); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
sesoi   <- 0.15

# ---- 1. Filter to first responses, then pre-standardize on the FULL first-response sample ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

responses_first <- responses |>
  dplyr::filter(response_order == 1) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
    # gender, public_sector_experience, pro_netanyahu_coalition kept as-is (binary; Gelman convention)
  )

# ---- 2. Formulas (no random intercept: each pid contributes one row) ----
f_basic_all <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition
f_full_all  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim + pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy

f_basic_sub <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim
f_full_sub  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy

f_int_basic <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition
f_int_full  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy

# ---- 3. Data subsets ----
dat_all  <- responses_first
dat_anti <- responses_first |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_first |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 4. Helpers (90% CIs to match pre-registered one-tailed alpha = .05) ----
fit_extract_main <- function(formula, data, group_label, spec_label) {
  m <- lm(formula, data = data)
  broom::tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = group_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_interaction <- function(formula, data, spec_label) {
  m <- lm(formula, data = data)
  broom::tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition", spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 5. Fit & extract ----
main_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_all, dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_all,  dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Main effect of populism")

int_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_basic, dat_all, "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_full,  dat_all, "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Interaction (full sample)")

plot_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(main_results, int_results) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    group = factor(group,
                   levels = c("Entire sample", "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                              "Netanyahu coalition", "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")),
    spec  = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Main effect of populism", "Interaction (full sample)"))
  )

# ---- 6. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "Sample restricted to each participant's first response (response_order == 1); one row per participant.",
  "OLS (no random intercept needed). 90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
  "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 7. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = group,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec,
                    group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                "With controls"    = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.30, 0.30)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" (90% CI) on response direction (first response only)"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```



multiplication

```{r}

library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication"
sesoi   <- 0.15

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample (so subgroup betas share a denominator) ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                            = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication  = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication),
    age_group                                     = z(age_group),
    education                                     = z(education),
    income                                        = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status             = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology                           = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization                        = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                             = z(external_efficecy)
    # gender, public_sector_experience, pro_netanyahu_coalition kept as-is (binary; Gelman convention)
  )

# ---- 2. Formulas ----
f_basic_all <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication + pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_full_all  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication + pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

f_basic_sub <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication + (1 | pid)
f_full_sub  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

f_int_basic <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition + (1 | pid)
f_int_full  <- response_direction ~ populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication * pro_netanyahu_coalition +
                                    gender + age_group + education + income +
                                    subjective_social_economic_status + public_sector_experience +
                                    right_left_ideology + affective_polarization + external_efficecy +
                                    (1 | pid)

# ---- 3. Data subsets ----
dat_all  <- responses_std
dat_anti <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 4. Helpers (90% CIs to match pre-registered one-tailed alpha = .05) ----
fit_extract_main <- function(formula, data, group_label, spec_label) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = group_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

fit_extract_interaction <- function(formula, data, spec_label) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")) |>
    dplyr::transmute(group = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition", spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

# ---- 5. Fit & extract ----
main_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_all, dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_all,  dat_all,  "Entire sample",         "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  "With controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_basic_sub, dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_main(f_full_sub,  dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Main effect of populism (multiplicative index)")

int_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_basic, dat_all, "Without controls"),
  fit_extract_interaction(f_int_full,  dat_all, "With controls")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(panel = "Interaction (full sample)")

plot_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(main_results, int_results) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    group = factor(group,
                   levels = c("Entire sample", "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                              "Netanyahu coalition", "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")),
    spec  = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Main effect of populism (multiplicative index)",
                                     "Interaction (full sample)"))
  )

# ---- 6. Caption ----
plot_caption <- paste(
  "Robustness check: populism operationalized as the multiplicative 4-dimension index",
  "(populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication), rather than the additive index.",
  "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
  "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
  "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
  "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 7. Plot ----
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = group,
                    linetype = spec, shape = spec,
                    group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
           ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                   "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                "With controls"    = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.25, 0.25)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = expression("Standardized "*beta*" (90% CI) on response direction"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = plot_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```

# Prevelance of different dimensions by populist attitudes


```{r}
# ============================================================================
# SHARED PREAMBLE
# ============================================================================
library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr); library(purrr)

pop_var   <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
all_dims  <- c("popular_sovereignty", "anti_elitism", "homogeneity", "manichean")
sesoi     <- 0.15
ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

# Standardize predictors only; warmth/competence stay 0/1 so LPM betas read as probability deltas
responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    popular_sovereignty               = z(popular_sovereignty),
    anti_elitism                      = z(anti_elitism),
    homogeneity                       = z(homogeneity),
    manichean                         = z(manichean),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
  )

dat_all  <- responses_std
dat_anti <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_std |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================================
# PLOT 1 — Headline: 4-dim populism index → P(warmth) and P(competence)
# ============================================================================

# Formula builders parameterized by DV
f_basic_all <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", "(1 | pid)"), dv)
f_full_all  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
f_basic_sub <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
f_full_sub  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
f_int_basic <- function(dv) reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), "(1 | pid)"), dv)
f_int_full  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)

extract_main <- function(data, panel_label, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
```
