```{r}
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}
extract_int <- function(data, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
  pattern <- paste0("^", pop_var, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

run_outcome <- function(dv, outcome_label) {
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    extract_main(dat_all,  "Entire sample",         outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_all(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_all,  "Entire sample",         outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_all(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_sub(dv)),
    extract_int( dat_all,                           outcome_label, "Without controls", f_int_basic(dv)),
    extract_int( dat_all,                           outcome_label, "With controls",    f_int_full(dv))
  )
}

results_p1 <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  run_outcome("warmth",     "Warmth"),
  run_outcome("competence", "Competence")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence")),
    spec    = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel   = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
  )

caption_p1 <- paste(
  "Outcomes: response-level binary indicators (0/1) for warmth and competence content.",
  "Linear probability models; coefficients = probability change in content per 1-SD populism.",
  "90% CIs (pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05). Shaded band: SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15 applied as a probability benchmark.",
  "Controls: gender, age, education, income, subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

ggplot(results_p1, aes(x = estimate, y = outcome,
                       linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi), linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18, position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid", "With controls" = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values    = c("Without controls" = 16,      "With controls" = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = expression("LPM "*beta*" (90% CI): "*Delta*"P(content) per 1-SD populism"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = caption_p1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================================
# PLOT 2 — Dimension-decomposed: 4 populism subdims → P(warmth) and P(competence)
# ============================================================================

# Formula builders parameterized by DV and focal dimension
fd_basic_all <- function(dv, dim) reformulate(c(dim, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", "(1 | pid)"), dv)
fd_full_all  <- function(dv, dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(dim, others, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
}
fd_basic_sub <- function(dv, dim) reformulate(c(dim, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
fd_full_sub  <- function(dv, dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(dim, others, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
}
fd_int_basic <- function(dv, dim) reformulate(c(paste0(dim, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), "(1 | pid)"), dv)
fd_int_full  <- function(dv, dim) {
  others <- setdiff(all_dims, dim)
  reformulate(c(paste0(dim, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), others, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"), dv)
}

extract_dim_main <- function(dim, data, dv, panel_label, outcome_label, spec_label, build_formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(build_formula(dv, dim), data = data, REML = TRUE)
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == dim) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, outcome = outcome_label,
                     dimension = dim, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}
extract_dim_int <- function(dim, data, dv, outcome_label, spec_label, build_formula) {
  m <- lmerTest::lmer(build_formula(dv, dim), data = data, REML = TRUE)
  pattern <- paste0("^", dim, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                    conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     outcome = outcome_label, dimension = dim, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

run_dim_outcome <- function(dv, outcome_label) {
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_all, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Entire sample",        outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = fd_basic_all),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_all, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Entire sample",        outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "With controls",    build_formula = fd_full_all),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_anti, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = fd_basic_sub),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_anti, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Anti-Netanyahu/Other", outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "With controls",    build_formula = fd_full_sub),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_net, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Netanyahu coalition",  outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "Without controls", build_formula = fd_basic_sub),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_main, data = dat_net, dv = dv,
                   panel_label = "Netanyahu coalition",  outcome_label = outcome_label,
                   spec_label = "With controls",    build_formula = fd_full_sub),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_int,  data = dat_all, dv = dv,
                   outcome_label = outcome_label, spec_label = "Without controls",
                   build_formula = fd_int_basic),
    purrr::map_dfr(all_dims, extract_dim_int,  data = dat_all, dv = dv,
                   outcome_label = outcome_label, spec_label = "With controls",
                   build_formula = fd_int_full)
  )
}

dim_labels <- c(popular_sovereignty = "Popular sovereignty",
                anti_elitism        = "Anti-elitism",
                homogeneity         = "Homogeneity",
                manichean           = "Manichean")

results_p2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  run_dim_outcome("warmth",     "Warmth"),
  run_dim_outcome("competence", "Competence")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = factor(dimension, levels = all_dims, labels = dim_labels[all_dims]),
    outcome   = factor(outcome,   levels = c("Warmth", "Competence")),
    spec      = factor(spec,      levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel     = factor(panel,     levels = panel_levels)
  )

caption_p2 <- paste(
  "Outcomes: response-level binary indicators (0/1) for warmth and competence content.",
  "Linear probability models; coefficients = probability change in content per 1-SD dimension.",
  "90% CIs (pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05). Shaded band: SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15 as a probability benchmark.",
  "'With controls' models adjust for the other three populism dimensions plus:",
  "gender, age, education, income, subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

ggplot(results_p2, aes(x = estimate, y = dimension,
                       linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi), linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18, position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid", "With controls" = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values    = c("Without controls" = 16,      "With controls" = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(panel), cols = vars(outcome), switch = "y") +
  labs(x = expression("LPM "*beta*" (90% CI): "*Delta*"P(content) per 1-SD dimension"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = caption_p2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", angle = 0, hjust = 0),
        strip.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 10, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```



## Anti elitism coefficient plots

```{r}
library(lme4); library(lmerTest); library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

sesoi <- 0.15
ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

# ---- 1. Pre-standardize on the FULL sample (all candidate predictors) ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))
responses_std <- responses |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    response_direction                            = z(response_direction),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim                 = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    anti_elitism                                  = z(anti_elitism),
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication  = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication),
    age_group                                     = z(age_group),
    education                                     = z(education),
    income                                        = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status             = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology                           = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization                        = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                             = z(external_efficecy)
  )

labels_vec <- c("public service employee",
                "public service bureaucrat",
                "public service clerk")

label_pretty <- c(
  "public service employee"   = "Public service employee",
  "public service bureaucrat" = "Public service bureaucrat",
  "public service clerk"      = "Public service clerk"
)

panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 2. Plot-builder function parameterized by predictor ----
make_label_plot <- function(pop_var, predictor_label) {

  # Formulas built dynamically from pop_var
  f_basic_all <- reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")
  f_full_all  <- reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")
  f_basic_sub <- reformulate(c(pop_var, "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")
  f_full_sub  <- reformulate(c(pop_var, ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")
  f_int_basic <- reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")
  f_int_full  <- reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), ctrl_vars, "(1 | pid)"),
                             response = "response_direction")

  # Subsets
  make_label_subsets <- function(label) {
    list(
      all  = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label),
      anti = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label,
                                            pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other"),
      net  = responses_std |> dplyr::filter(civil_servant_label == label,
                                            pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")
    )
  }
  subs <- stats::setNames(lapply(labels_vec, make_label_subsets), labels_vec)

  # Helpers
  fit_extract_main <- function(data, panel_label, label_label, spec_label, formula) {
    m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
    broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                      conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
      dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
      dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, label = label_label, spec = spec_label,
                       estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
  }
  fit_extract_int <- function(data, label_label, spec_label, formula) {
    m <- lmerTest::lmer(formula, data = data, REML = TRUE)
    pattern <- paste0("^", pop_var, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
    broom.mixed::tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE,
                      conf.method = "Wald", conf.level = 0.90) |>
      dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                       label = label_label, spec = spec_label,
                       estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
  }

  fit_one_label <- function(label) {
    s <- subs[[label]]
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      fit_extract_main(s$all,  "Entire sample",         label, "Without controls", f_basic_all),
      fit_extract_main(s$all,  "Entire sample",         label, "With controls",    f_full_all),
      fit_extract_main(s$anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
      fit_extract_main(s$anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  label, "With controls",    f_full_sub),
      fit_extract_main(s$net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub),
      fit_extract_main(s$net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   label, "With controls",    f_full_sub),
      fit_extract_int(s$all,                            label, "Without controls", f_int_basic),
      fit_extract_int(s$all,                            label, "With controls",    f_int_full)
    )
  }

  results <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(labels_vec, fit_one_label))

  plot_df <- results |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      label = factor(label, levels = labels_vec, labels = label_pretty[labels_vec]),
      spec  = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
      panel = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
    )

  plot_caption <- paste(
    "Each panel is fit separately within respondents assigned to each civil-servant label condition.",
    "90% CIs correspond to the pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05.",
    "Shaded band: pre-registered SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15.",
    "Controls in 'With controls' models: gender, age, education, income,",
    "subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
    "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
    sep = "\n"
  )

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = label,
                      linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
    annotate("rect",
             xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi,
             ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,
             fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi),
               linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                   height = 0.18,
                   position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid",
                                     "With controls"    = "dashed")) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Without controls" = 16,
                                  "With controls"    = 21)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
    facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
    labs(x = bquote("Standardized "*beta*" of "*.(predictor_label)*" on response direction (90% CI)"),
         y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
         caption = plot_caption) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
          plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
          plot.caption.position = "plot")
}

# ---- 3. Run for each predictor ----
p_anti <- make_label_plot(
  pop_var         = "anti_elitism",
  predictor_label = "anti-elitism"
)

p_mult <- make_label_plot(
  pop_var         = "populist_attitudes_index_4dim_multiplication",
  predictor_label = "populism (multiplicative)"
)

# Display them
p_anti
p_mult
```






## Common traits by coalition × populism — valence × dimension




For each of the four groups defined by coalition (Anti-Netanyahu/Other vs.
Netanyahu coalition) crossed with a median split on the multidimensional
populist attitudes index, the most frequently mentioned traits are placed
along a negative-to-positive valence continuum (mean `response_direction`).
Text size encodes frequency; color marks the content dimension
(warmth / competence / both / other). All civil-servant labels are pooled.


```{r common-traits-build-popcoal}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# 0. 4-group variable: coalition × populism (median split) ----------------
# Median is computed across unique respondents so the threshold is not
# weighted by per-respondent response counts.

median_pop <- responses %>%
  dplyr::distinct(pid, populist_attitudes_index_4dim) %>%
  dplyr::pull(populist_attitudes_index_4dim) %>%
  median(na.rm = TRUE)

responses <- responses %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pop_level = ifelse(populist_attitudes_index_4dim >= median_pop,
                       "High populism", "Low populism"),
    pop_x_coalition = factor(
      paste(pro_netanyahu_coalition, pop_level, sep = " / "),
      levels = c("Anti-Netanyahu/Other / Low populism",
                 "Anti-Netanyahu/Other / High populism",
                 "Netanyahu coalition / Low populism",
                 "Netanyahu coalition / High populism")
    )
  )

# 1. English translations -------------------------------------------------
# Two-tier translation lookup. Tier (a) pulls the per-response `translation`
# column from `coded_responses_train.csv`. Tier (b) is a manual override for
# frequent Hebrew responses that were missing from (a) or that needed a
# clearer English gloss. Manual overrides always win.

translations_csv <- readr::read_csv("coded_responses_train.csv",
                                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::transmute(response,
                   response_en = translation) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_en),
                response_en != "NA",
                stringr::str_squish(response_en) != "") |>
  dplyr::count(response, response_en, sort = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::group_by(response) |>
  dplyr::slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(response, response_en)

# Manual overrides — extend this list whenever an untranslated Hebrew term
# shows up in the table or plot.
translations_manual <- tibble::tribble(
  ~response,        ~response_en,
  "תנאים",           "(work) conditions",
  "תנאים טובים",      "good (work) conditions",
  "סחבת",            "foot-dragging",
  "מסורבל",          "cumbersome",
  "טרחנים",          "fussy",
  "עייפות",          "tiredness",
  "נחמדים",          "nice",
  "שירות",           "service",
  "לא יודע",         "don't know",
  "לא יודעת",        "don't know"
)

translations <- translations_csv |>
  dplyr::anti_join(translations_manual, by = "response") |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(translations_manual)

# 2. Trait-level summary --------------------------------------------------
# Translate first, then group by the English trait so that morphological
# variants of the same word collapse into a single row. The original Hebrew
# variants are preserved as a comma-separated list. Dimension is the modal
# content code across the underlying response-level rows.

mode_value <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  tab <- table(x)
  names(tab)[which.max(tab)]
}

trait_summary <- responses %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = dplyr::case_when(
      warmth == 1 & competence == 1 ~ "Both",
      warmth == 1                   ~ "Warmth",
      competence == 1               ~ "Competence",
      TRUE                          ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(translations, by = "response") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(response_en = dplyr::coalesce(response_en, response)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(pop_x_coalition)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pop_x_coalition, response_en) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n              = dplyr::n(),
    mean_direction = mean(response_direction, na.rm = TRUE),
    dimension      = mode_value(dimension),
    responses_he   = paste(sort(unique(response)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dimension = factor(dimension,
                       levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Both", "Other"))
  )

# 3. Keep the top-N traits per group for readable plot/table output -------
top_n_traits <- 30

top_traits <- trait_summary %>%
  dplyr::group_by(pop_x_coalition) %>%
  dplyr::slice_max(n, n = top_n_traits, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()
```

### Table — most common traits by coalition × populism group

```{r common-traits-table-popcoal}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

top_traits %>%
  dplyr::arrange(pop_x_coalition, dplyr::desc(n)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Group`           = pop_x_coalition,
    `Trait (English)` = response_en,
    `Trait (Hebrew)`  = responses_he,
    Dimension         = dimension,
    Frequency         = n,
    `Mean valence`    = round(mean_direction, 2)
  ) %>%
  gt::gt(groupname_col = "Group") %>%
  gt::tab_header(
    title    = gt::md("**Most common traits by coalition \u00D7 populist-attitudes group**"),
    subtitle = gt::md(paste0("Top ", top_n_traits,
                             " traits per group, sorted by frequency.",
                             " *Mean valence* averages `response_direction`",
                             " (lower = more negative, higher = more positive).",
                             " Median split on `populist_attitudes_index_4dim`."))
  ) %>%
  gt::cols_align("left",   columns = c(`Trait (English)`, `Trait (Hebrew)`,
                                       Dimension)) %>%
  gt::cols_align("right",  columns = c(Frequency, `Mean valence`)) %>%
  gt::data_color(
    columns = `Mean valence`,
    palette = c("#B2182B", "#F4A582", "#F7F7F7", "#92C5DE", "#2166AC")
  )
```

First response

```{r}
# ============================================================================
# PLOT 1 (first response only) — 4-dim populism index → P(warmth), P(competence)
# ============================================================================
library(broom); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(stringr)

pop_var   <- "populist_attitudes_index_4dim"
sesoi     <- 0.15
ctrl_vars <- c("gender", "age_group", "education", "income",
               "subjective_social_economic_status", "public_sector_experience",
               "right_left_ideology", "affective_polarization", "external_efficecy")

panel_levels <- c("Entire sample",
                  "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",
                  "Netanyahu coalition",
                  "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 1. Filter to first responses, then standardize within this subset ----
z <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

responses_first <- responses |>
  dplyr::filter(response_order == 1) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    populist_attitudes_index_4dim     = z(populist_attitudes_index_4dim),
    age_group                         = z(age_group),
    education                         = z(education),
    income                            = z(income),
    subjective_social_economic_status = z(subjective_social_economic_status),
    right_left_ideology               = z(right_left_ideology),
    affective_polarization            = z(affective_polarization),
    external_efficecy                 = z(external_efficecy)
    # warmth, competence kept as 0/1 (LPM betas read as probability deltas)
    # gender, public_sector_experience, pro_netanyahu_coalition kept as-is
  )

dat_all  <- responses_first
dat_anti <- responses_first |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Anti-Netanyahu/Other")
dat_net  <- responses_first |> dplyr::filter(pro_netanyahu_coalition == "Netanyahu coalition")

# ---- 2. Formula builders parameterized by DV (no random intercept) ----
f_basic_all <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition"), dv)
f_full_all  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, "pro_netanyahu_coalition", ctrl_vars), dv)
f_basic_sub <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var), dv)
f_full_sub  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(pop_var, ctrl_vars), dv)
f_int_basic <- function(dv) reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition")), dv)
f_int_full  <- function(dv) reformulate(c(paste0(pop_var, " * pro_netanyahu_coalition"), ctrl_vars), dv)

# ---- 3. Helpers (90% CIs to match pre-registered one-tailed alpha = .05) ----
extract_main <- function(data, panel_label, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lm(formula, data = data)
  broom::tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(term == pop_var) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = panel_label, outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}
extract_int <- function(data, outcome_label, spec_label, formula) {
  m <- lm(formula, data = data)
  pattern <- paste0("^", pop_var, ":pro_netanyahu_coalition")
  broom::tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90) |>
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(term, pattern)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(panel = "Populism \u00D7 Netanyahu coalition",
                     outcome = outcome_label, spec = spec_label,
                     estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
}

run_outcome <- function(dv, outcome_label) {
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    extract_main(dat_all,  "Entire sample",         outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_all(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_all,  "Entire sample",         outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_all(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_anti, "Anti-Netanyahu/Other",  outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   outcome_label, "Without controls", f_basic_sub(dv)),
    extract_main(dat_net,  "Netanyahu coalition",   outcome_label, "With controls",    f_full_sub(dv)),
    extract_int( dat_all,                           outcome_label, "Without controls", f_int_basic(dv)),
    extract_int( dat_all,                           outcome_label, "With controls",    f_int_full(dv))
  )
}

# ---- 4. Fit & extract ----
results_p1 <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  run_outcome("warmth",     "Warmth"),
  run_outcome("competence", "Competence")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence")),
    spec    = factor(spec, levels = c("Without controls", "With controls")),
    panel   = factor(panel, levels = panel_levels)
  )

# ---- 5. Caption ----
caption_p1 <- paste(
  "Sample restricted to each participant's first response (response_order == 1); one row per participant.",
  "Outcomes: binary indicators (0/1) for warmth and competence content. OLS linear probability models.",
  "Coefficients = probability change in content per 1-SD populism.",
  "90% CIs (pre-registered one-tailed \u03B1 = .05). Shaded band: SESOI of |\u03B2| < 0.15 as a probability benchmark.",
  "Controls: gender, age, education, income, subjective socio-economic status, public-sector experience,",
  "left\u2013right ideology, affective polarization, external political efficacy.",
  sep = "\n"
)

# ---- 6. Plot ----
ggplot(results_p1, aes(x = estimate, y = outcome,
                       linetype = spec, shape = spec, group = spec)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -sesoi, xmax = sesoi, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey85", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-sesoi, sesoi), linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.18, position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, fill = "white", colour = "#1f3552",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.55)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Without controls" = "solid", "With controls" = "dashed")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values    = c("Without controls" = 16,      "With controls" = 21)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 1) +
  labs(x = expression("LPM "*beta*" (90% CI): "*Delta*"P(content) per 1-SD populism (first response only)"),
       y = NULL, linetype = NULL, shape = NULL,
       caption = caption_p1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 11, lineheight = 1.15),
        plot.caption.position = "plot")
```



### Plot — traits along the valence continuum

```{r common-traits-plot-popcoal}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 12

# ggrepel keeps the trait labels from overlapping; text size encodes
# frequency, x-position encodes mean valence (negative → positive).

set.seed(42)  # ggrepel uses a randomised layout

ggplot(top_traits,
       aes(x = mean_direction,
           y = 0,
           label = response_en,
           size  = n,
           color = dimension)) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    max.overlaps      = Inf,
    box.padding       = 0.45,
    point.padding     = 0.05,
    segment.color     = NA,
    force             = 2,
    seed              = 42
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(pop_x_coalition), switch = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name   = "Mean response direction (negative \u2192 positive)",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.05))
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(name  = "Frequency",
                        range = c(3, 8)) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Content dimension",
    values = c("Warmth"     = "#E07B91",
               "Competence" = "#4C9EBE",
               "Both"       = "#7E57C2",
               "Other"      = "grey55"),
    drop   = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Most common traits by coalition \u00D7 populist-attitudes group",
    subtitle = paste0("Top ", top_n_traits,
                      " traits per group arranged along the valence continuum;",
                      " text size = frequency. Median split on populist_attitudes_index_4dim;",
                      " civil-servant labels pooled."),
    caption  = "Source: Wave 2 open-ended trait responses."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y      = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    strip.placement   = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    legend.position   = "bottom"
  ) +
  guides(
    size  = guide_legend(override.aes = list(label = "abc")),
    color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(label = "abc", size = 5))
  )
```


```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(scales)

# ---- 1. Build the per-response classification ----
plot_dat <- responses |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(response_direction), erase != 1) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    direction_cat = dplyr::case_when(
      response_direction <= 2 ~ "Negative",
      response_direction == 3 ~ "Neutral",
      response_direction >= 4 ~ "Positive"
    ),
    direction_cat = factor(direction_cat, levels = c("Negative", "Neutral", "Positive")),
    is_warmth     = warmth == 1,
    is_competence = competence == 1,
    is_other      = !is_warmth & !is_competence
  )

total_n <- nrow(plot_dat)

# ---- 2. Long format: a response can appear in both Warmth and Competence ----
# (Inclusive grouping; Warmth + Competence + Other may sum to >100% of total
#  because warmth and competence are not mutually exclusive at the response level.)
group_dat <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  plot_dat |> dplyr::filter(is_warmth)     |> dplyr::mutate(group = "Warmth"),
  plot_dat |> dplyr::filter(is_competence) |> dplyr::mutate(group = "Competence"),
  plot_dat |> dplyr::filter(is_other)      |> dplyr::mutate(group = "Other")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Other")))

# ---- 3. Counts and proportions ----
counts <- group_dat |>
  dplyr::count(group, direction_cat, name = "n") |>
  dplyr::group_by(group) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_in_group = sum(n),
    pct_within     = n / total_in_group,
    prop_of_total  = n / total_n
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

group_totals <- counts |>
  dplyr::group_by(group) |>
  dplyr::summarise(group_share = sum(prop_of_total), .groups = "drop")

# ---- 4. Plot ----
ggplot(counts, aes(x = prop_of_total, y = group, fill = direction_cat)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct_within, accuracy = 1)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5, reverse = TRUE),
            size = 3.6, fontface = "bold", colour = "white") +
  geom_text(data = group_totals,
            aes(x = group_share, y = group,
                label = paste0(" ", scales::percent(group_share, accuracy = 1), " of all responses")),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            hjust = 0, size = 3.4, colour = "grey25") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Negative" = "#F08080",   # red
               "Neutral"  = "#F0D87B",   # gold
               "Positive" = "#3F88B5"),  # blue
    name = "Response direction"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    name   = "Share of all responses",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.18))
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  labs(y = NULL,
       title    = "Response distribution by content dimension",
       subtitle = "Bar length = share of all responses; within-bar segments split by valence",
       caption  = "Within-bar percentages sum to 100% per dimension. A response can be tagged for both warmth and competence, so the three bars may sum to more than 100%.") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position    = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
        plot.caption       = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 10, colour = "grey40"))
```



```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(scales); library(forcats)

# ---- 1. Build per-response classification ----
plot_dat <- responses |>
  filter(!is.na(response_direction), erase != 1) |>
  mutate(
    direction_cat = case_when(
      response_direction <= 2 ~ "Negative",
      response_direction == 3 ~ "Neutral",
      response_direction >= 4 ~ "Positive"
    ),
    direction_cat = factor(direction_cat,
                           levels = c("Negative", "Neutral", "Positive")),
    is_warmth     = warmth == 1,
    is_competence = competence == 1,
    is_other      = !is_warmth & !is_competence
  )

total_n <- nrow(plot_dat)

# ---- 2. Long format (inclusive grouping) ----
group_dat <- bind_rows(
  plot_dat |> filter(is_warmth)     |> mutate(group = "Warmth"),
  plot_dat |> filter(is_competence) |> mutate(group = "Competence"),
  plot_dat |> filter(is_other)      |> mutate(group = "Other")
) |>
  mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("Warmth", "Competence", "Other")))

# ---- 3. Counts ----
counts <- group_dat |>
  count(group, direction_cat, name = "n") |>
  group_by(group) |>
  mutate(
    total_in_group = sum(n),
    pct_within     = n / total_in_group,
    prop_of_total  = n / total_n
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(group_label = paste0(group, "\n(n = ", comma(total_in_group), ")"),
         group_label = factor(group_label,
                              levels = unique(group_label[order(group)])))

group_totals <- counts |>
  group_by(group_label) |>
  summarise(group_share = sum(prop_of_total), .groups = "drop")

# ---- 4. Plot ----
ggplot(counts, aes(x = prop_of_total, y = fct_rev(group_label),
                   fill = direction_cat)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78, position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE),
           color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(pct_within >= 0.05,
                               percent(pct_within, accuracy = 1), "")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5, reverse = TRUE),
            size = 5, fontface = "bold", colour = "white",
            family = "Open Sans") +
  geom_text(data = group_totals,
            aes(x = group_share, y = fct_rev(group_label),
                label = paste0("  ", percent(group_share, accuracy = 1),
                               " of all responses")),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            hjust = 0, size = 4.2, fontface = "bold",
            colour = "#1F3343", family = "Open Sans") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Negative" = "#C44536",   # deep red
               "Neutral"  = "#BFBFBF",   # gray
               "Positive" = "#2E86AB"),  # deep blue
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.25))
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete() +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title    = "Spontaneous stereotypes split fairly evenly across SCM dimensions",
    subtitle = "Bar length = share of all responses. Within-bar segments = valence split.",
    caption  = "Within-bar percentages sum to 100% per dimension. A response can be coded for both warmth and competence,\nso the three rows may sum to more than 100%.",
    x = "Share of all responses",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16, base_family = "Open Sans") +
  theme(
    panel.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background     = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y  = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor    = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x  = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    plot.title          = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#1F3343",
                                       size = 18, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle       = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey45",
                                       margin = margin(b = 14)),
    plot.caption        = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 9,
                                       color = "grey50", lineheight = 1.1,
                                       margin = margin(t = 10)),
    legend.position     = "top",
    legend.justification = c(0, 0),
    legend.text         = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.key.size     = unit(0.45, "cm"),
    legend.box.spacing  = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    axis.title.x        = element_text(size = 12, color = "grey40",
                                       margin = margin(t = 8)),
    axis.text.y         = element_text(size = 13, color = "#1F3343",
                                       face = "bold", lineheight = 0.9,
                                       hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x         = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey45"),
    plot.margin         = margin(15, 30, 15, 15)
  )
```
